front: What is the role of amino acid-derived neurotransmitters?
back: Glutamate (excitatory) and GABA (inhibitory) are the main CNS neurotransmitters. Glycine is inhibitory in spinal cord. Histamine (from histidine) modulates wakefulness. Serotonin (from tryptophan) regulates mood. Dopamine (from tyrosine) controls reward.
